architecture_summary: Add a Foundation Mode closure summary that consumes the runtime promotion decision and records the read-only worker runtime chain as closed for Foundation Mode while live runtime remains blocked.
data_model_changes: Introduce a closure summary schema and fixture with SolvedVerified outcome, proof_state Pass, complete chain refs, and explicit live-runtime blockers.

Preserve accepted evidence refs from the promotion decision while keeping runtime promotion, admission, enablement, dispatch, worker invocation, receipt emission, receipt append, and live terminal closure denied.
Register the schema in the protocol manifest, schema index, CI worker lane, docs, and SDLC artifacts.
implementation_plan: Add closure summary schema, fixture, validator, and focused tests.
Register the schema in the protocol manifest and CI worker runtime enablement lane.
Update worker docs, schema README, gap register, and SDLC artifacts.
Run focused validator, focused pytest, protocol manifest validation, SDLC artifact validation, schema validation, and whitespace checks.
invariants: Foundation closure is not live terminal closure.
Dispatch, worker invocation, receipt emission, receipt append, and success claims remain denied.
The full Foundation runtime chain remains explicitly referenced.
rollback_plan: Remove closure summary schema, fixture, validator, tests, manifest entry, CI inclusion, docs updates, and SDLC artifacts.
Restore the worker runtime next action to stopping after the Foundation Mode promotion denial.
tradeoffs: The slice closes the Foundation proof thread without enabling runtime.
Any future live runtime thread remains separate and requires explicit non-Foundation authority.
